bytes.topic: Bitcoin — UTXO model, Script, PoW, block structure, Lightning Network
bytes.title: Cosmos — Integrating Bitcoin UTXO Model with Cosmos-SDK
bytes.body: Cosmos developers can leverage the Bitcoin UTXO model by utilizing the Cosmos-SDK's AnteHandler to simulate UTXO transactions. The AnteHandler allows for custom transaction handling, enabling the integration of UTXO-based logic. To achieve this, developers can create a custom AnteHandler that checks for UTXO-specific conditions, such as input/output validation and transaction sequencing. The Cosmos-SDK's banking module can then be used to manage the UTXO-based transactions, ensuring seamless interaction with the Cosmos blockchain. By integrating the UTXO model, Cosmos developers can expand their blockchain's functionality and interoperability with other UTXO-based chains, such as Bitcoin.

bytes.topic: Solana — account model, PoH + PoS, Sealevel parallel execution, programs vs accounts
bytes.title: Cosmos Developers: Mastering Solana's Account Model and Parallel Execution
bytes.body: Solana's account model is based on a simple key-value store, where each account has a unique address and can store data. The network uses a combination of Proof of History (PoH) and Proof of Stake (PoS) consensus algorithms to secure the network. Sealevel parallel execution enables the network to process multiple transactions in parallel, increasing throughput. In Solana, programs and accounts are distinct concepts: programs are stateless and execute transactions, while accounts store data. Developers can use the Solana Web3.js library to interact with the network and build custom applications.

bytes.topic: Cosmos — Tendermint consensus, IBC protocol, app-specific chains, ABCI
bytes.title: Cosmos - Tendermint Consensus: Understanding Height and Block Proposal
bytes.body: Tendermint consensus in Cosmos relies on a leader-based protocol where each validator node proposes a block. The proposed block undergoes validation and is committed to the blockchain once a majority of validators agree. Height, represented by the `Height` struct in `tendermint/types`, is the primary identifier for a block. It's a 64-bit integer incremented after each block proposal. The `Height` struct also contains a `ChainID` field, which helps identify the specific Cosmos chain. When creating a block proposal, use the `CreateBlock` function from `cosmos-sdk/types` to specify the block's height and chain ID. This ensures the proposal is valid and can be committed to the blockchain.

bytes.topic: Polkadot — relay chain, parachains, XCM cross-chain messaging, parachain slots
bytes.title: Cosmos: Harnessing Polkadot's Parachains with IBC

bytes.topic: Bitcoin Script — stack-based, intentionally limited, P2PKH, P2SH, multisig
bytes.title: Cosmos: Mastering Bitcoin Script for Secure Non-EVM Transactions
bytes.body: Bitcoin Script is a stack-based, intentionally limited scripting language used for transaction verification. In Cosmos' non-EVM chains, it's crucial to understand its fundamentals. Two primary types of transactions are P2PKH (Pay-to-Public-Key-Hash) and P2SH (Pay-to-Script-Hash). P2PKH involves directly sending funds to a public key, while P2SH sends funds to a hash of a script, providing an additional layer of security. Multisignature transactions, which require multiple signatures to authorize transactions, are also supported. To create a secure P2SH transaction in Cosmos, you'll need to create a redeem script and hash it. The redeem script can include multiple signatures, ensuring that multiple parties must sign off before the funds can be spent.
